message:
fix: portal-wrap war-stories overlay to escape transform stacking context

The War Stories detail overlay was rendering inside a parent that uses
CSS transforms (via Framer Motion), which creates a new stacking
context. This caused the "fixed" overlay to be positioned relative to
the transformed ancestor instead of the viewport, breaking the
full-screen modal appearance.

Solution has two parts:

1. Add a Portal component to motion-wrapper.tsx that uses React's
   createPortal() to render children at document.body. It gates on
   a mounted state via useEffect to avoid SSR hydration mismatches.

2. Wrap the war-stories detail overlay (<AnimatePresence> modal) in
   <Portal>, moving its DOM node to body where "position: fixed"
   behaves correctly relative to the viewport. The overlay's click
   handlers, z-index (999), and backdrop-blur remain unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
